edges.description: Mutagenic injury selects for loss of p53-mediated genomic surveillance
Loss of p53 checkpoint function enables unchecked cell-cycle progression
Squamous lineage transcription factors reinforce malignant growth programs
Cell-cycle dysregulation cooperates with oncogenic survival signaling
Loss of normal squamous differentiation signaling promotes malignant progression
SOX2 can activate growth-promoting AKT/mTORC1 signaling
Squamous lineage oncogene activation promotes ESCC growth
PI3K/AKT activity promotes growth and survival of ESCC cells
Impaired squamous differentiation favors tumor expansion
Expanding tumors acquire PD-L1-linked immune escape programs.
Tumor growth progressively narrows the esophageal lumen and impairs swallowing.
Tumor ulceration and local wall invasion can make swallowing painful.
Locally invasive or ulcerative esophageal tumor growth can produce retrosternal chest pain.
Ulcerative tumor growth can erode mucosa and cause upper gastrointestinal bleeding.
Tumor burden and impaired oral intake contribute to weight loss.
Chronic checkpoint signaling drives tumor-infiltrating T cells into an exhausted, non-productive state.
edges.target: T Cell Exhaustion and Immune Escape

nodes.description: A substantial subset of advanced ESCC expresses PD-L1 on tumor cells and tumor-infiltrating immune cells, consistent with a checkpoint-mediated immune-evasion state targetable with PD-1 blockade.

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell mediated immunity

evidence.reference_title: PD-L1 expression and its clinicopathological correlation in advanced esophageal squamous cell carcinoma in a Chinese population.
evidence.snippet: PD-L1 was expressed on 29.9% (113/378) ESCC tumor cells and 40.2% (152/378) tumor-infiltrating immune cells.

nodes.description: Chronic exposure to tobacco smoke and alcohol-derived acetaldehyde injures the esophageal squamous epithelium and promotes carcinogenesis through DNA damage, epigenetic disruption, and repeated mutagenic stress.

evidence.reference_title: Alcohol drinking, cigarette smoking, and the development of squamous cell carcinoma of the esophagus: molecular mechanisms of carcinogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Both alcohol consumption and cigarette smoking are major risk factors for the development of ESCC.

evidence.reference_title: Alcohol drinking, cigarette smoking, and the development of squamous cell carcinoma of the esophagus: molecular mechanisms of carcinogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Acetaldehyde is the most toxic ethanol metabolite in alcohol-associated carcinogenesis, while ethanol itself stimulates carcinogenesis by inhibiting DNA methylation and by interacting with retinoid metabolism.

nodes.description: Recurrent alteration of TP53, CCND1, CDKN2A, and related regulators disrupts G1/S control and promotes persistent proliferation of transformed esophageal squamous cells.

evidence.reference_title: Prognostic value of genomic mutation signature associated with immune microenvironment in southern Chinese patients with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Pathway analysis revealed dysregulation of cell cycle regulation, epigenetic regulation, PI3K/AKT signaling, and NOTCH signaling.

nodes.description: Retrosternal chest pain can accompany locally invasive or ulcerative esophageal tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Adding external beam to intra-luminal brachytherapy improves palliation in obstructive squamous cell oesophageal cancer: a prospective multi-centre randomized trial of the International Atomic Energy Agency.
evidence.snippet: In longitudinal regression analyses, scores for dysphagia (p=0.00005), odynophagia (p=0.006), regurgitation (p=0.00005), chest pain (p=0.0038) and performance status (p=0.0015) were all significantly improved.

nodes.description: Tumor ulceration may cause upper gastrointestinal bleeding with vomiting of blood.

evidence.reference_title: Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma Presenting with Streptococcus intermedius Cerebral Abscess.
evidence.snippet: We report the case of a 53-year-old female who presented with hematemesis and melena followed by left-sided weakness.

nodes.description: Convergent disruption of DNA damage control, cell-cycle regulation, squamous differentiation, and prosurvival signaling drives continued ESCC cell proliferation, invasion, and progression.

evidence.reference_title: Prognostic value of genomic mutation signature associated with immune microenvironment in southern Chinese patients with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Pathway analysis revealed dysregulation of cell cycle regulation, epigenetic regulation, PI3K/AKT signaling, and NOTCH signaling.

evidence.reference_title: SOX2 promotes tumor growth of esophageal squamous cell carcinoma through the AKT/mammalian target of rapamycin complex 1 signaling pathway.
evidence.snippet: In mouse xenografts, SOX2 promoted in vivo tumor growth of ESCC, which was dependent on AKT/mTORC1 activation.

nodes.description: Alteration of NOTCH pathway genes perturbs normal squamous differentiation programs and contributes to malignant transformation and progression in ESCC.

evidence.reference_title: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of the Most Common Genetic Mutations in Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.
Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of the Most Common Genetic Mutations in Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.

nodes.description: Anti-PD-1 immunotherapy used in advanced or previously treated ESCC. It improves overall survival compared with chemotherapy in the post-platinum setting and is now part of the immunotherapy treatment landscape for ESCC.

evidence.reference_title: Three-Year Follow-Up and Response-Survival Relationship of Nivolumab in Previously Treated Patients with Advanced Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma (ATTRACTION-3).
evidence.snippet: Nivolumab as second-line therapy demonstrated clinically meaningful long-term improvement in OS compared with chemotherapy in previously treated patients with advanced ESCC.

nodes.description: Painful swallowing may occur when the tumor ulcerates or deeply invades the esophageal wall.

evidence.reference_title: Adding external beam to intra-luminal brachytherapy improves palliation in obstructive squamous cell oesophageal cancer: a prospective multi-centre randomized trial of the International Atomic Energy Agency.
evidence.snippet: In longitudinal regression analyses, scores for dysphagia (p=0.00005), odynophagia (p=0.006), regurgitation (p=0.00005), chest pain (p=0.0038) and performance status (p=0.0015) were all significantly improved.

nodes.description: Recurrent PIK3CA and related pathway alterations increase prosurvival and growth signaling in ESCC, supporting tumor maintenance and therapeutic resistance.

evidence.reference_title: Prognostic value of genomic mutation signature associated with immune microenvironment in southern Chinese patients with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Pathway analysis revealed dysregulation of cell cycle regulation, epigenetic regulation, PI3K/AKT signaling, and NOTCH signaling.

nodes.description: Pembrolizumab-containing regimens are used in advanced ESCC, particularly in biomarker-selected settings and combination regimens involving chemotherapy.

evidence.reference_title: Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy versus chemotherapy alone for first-line treatment of advanced oesophageal cancer (KEYNOTE-590): a randomised, placebo-controlled, phase 3 study.
evidence.snippet: Compared with placebo plus chemotherapy, pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy improved overall survival in patients with previously untreated, advanced oesophageal squamous cell carcinoma and PD-L1 CPS of 10 or more, and overall survival and progression-free survival in patients with oesophageal squamous cell carcinoma, PD-L1 CPS of 10 or more, and in all randomised patients regardless of histology, and had a manageable safety profile in the total as-treated population.

nodes.description: Progressive difficulty swallowing is the most common presenting symptom as the luminal tumor narrows the esophagus.

evidence.reference_title: Delay in the diagnosis of esophageal carcinoma: experience of a single unit from a developing country.
evidence.snippet: First symptom was progressive dysphagia in all patients.

evidence.reference_title: Adding external beam to intra-luminal brachytherapy improves palliation in obstructive squamous cell oesophageal cancer: a prospective multi-centre randomized trial of the International Atomic Energy Agency.
evidence.snippet: In longitudinal regression analyses, scores for dysphagia (p=0.00005), odynophagia (p=0.006), regurgitation (p=0.00005), chest pain (p=0.0038) and performance status (p=0.0015) were all significantly improved.

evidence.reference_title: SOX2 antagonizes WWC1 to drive YAP1 activation in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Here, we reveal that SOX2, ACTL6A, and TP63 are co-amplified and upregulated in ESCC samples.

nodes.description: ESCC frequently shows 3q amplification with co-amplification and upregulation of SOX2 and TP63, reinforcing a malignant squamous lineage transcriptional program. SOX2 promotes tumor growth and interacts with oncogenic signaling programs including AKT/mTORC1 and YAP1.

evidence.reference_title: SOX2 antagonizes WWC1 to drive YAP1 activation in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Here, we reveal that SOX2, ACTL6A, and TP63 are co-amplified and upregulated in ESCC samples.

evidence.reference_title: SOX2 promotes tumor growth of esophageal squamous cell carcinoma through the AKT/mammalian target of rapamycin complex 1 signaling pathway.
evidence.snippet: SOX2 promotes ESCC cell proliferation in vitro.

nodes.description: In the immunosuppressive ESCC microenvironment, chronically stimulated tumor-infiltrating T cells acquire an exhausted phenotype with impaired effector cytokine production and cytolytic activity, contributing to immune escape.
nodes.id: T Cell Exhaustion and Immune Escape

evidence.reference_title: T-cell exhaustion in the tumor microenvironment.
evidence.snippet: The exhausted T cells in the tumor microenvironment show overexpressed inhibitory receptors, decreased effector cytokine production and cytolytic activity, leading to the failure of cancer elimination.

evidence.reference_title: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of the Most Common Genetic Mutations in Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.

nodes.description: TP53 alteration is the dominant recurrent genetic event in ESCC and compromises cell-cycle checkpoint control, apoptosis, and genome integrity. Loss of p53 function permits survival and expansion of damaged esophageal epithelial clones.

evidence.reference_title: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of the Most Common Genetic Mutations in Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma.
SOX2 antagonizes WWC1 to drive YAP1 activation in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Here, we reveal that SOX2, ACTL6A, and TP63 are co-amplified and upregulated in ESCC samples.

nodes.description: Weight loss is common because of impaired oral intake, cancer-associated catabolism, and advanced disease burden.

evidence.reference_title: Delay in the diagnosis of esophageal carcinoma: experience of a single unit from a developing country.
evidence.snippet: Subsidiary symptoms were, weight loss in 83.3% (n = 40), abdominal / chest pain in 10 (20.8%), regurgitation in 14 (29.2%), odynophagia in three (6.2%)
